title: HOME ACCESS AND REPAIR FOR THE DISABLED, ELDERLY - 211info
text: WASHINGTON COUNTY COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT HOME ACCESS AND REPAIR FOR THE DISABLED, ELDERLY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides grants of up to $10,000 for home repairs such as electrical wiring, heating, roofs, foundations, removal of decks and porches and accessibility modifications such as widening of doors, installation of ramps, walk-in/roll-in showers, grab bars, and hearing impaired smoke detectors. 328 W Main Street Suite 100 Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Homeowners age 62 or older or adults with disabilities who have less than $100,000 in assets. Applicants must live on on the property if applying for home repairs, renters are only eligible for accessibility modifications. Hours: Tuesday-Thursday 11am-2pm Email (503) 846-8814 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 846-8814 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Washington County (excluding City of Beaverton) SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: Home Rehabilitation Programs * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Home Rehabilitation Programs * Older Adults Other Services Offered At This Location: ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AND IMPROVEMENT - WOOD STOVE EXCHANGE PROGRAM HOUSING/SHELTER - HOUSING REHABILITATION LOANS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. WASHINGTON COUNTY COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT
